### MIAM exemption not validly claimed [ F55 or no longer applicable ] E+W


3.10. — [ F56 (1) If a MIAM exemption has been claimed, the court will inquire into whether the exemption—


(1A) The inquiry referred to in paragraph (1) must be made—


(2) If a court finds that the MIAM exemption was not validly claimed [ F57 , or that it was validly claimed but is no longer applicable ] , the court will—


unless the court considers that in all the circumstances of the case, the MIAM requirement should not apply to the application in question.


(3) In making a decision under Rule 3.10(2), the court will have particular regard to—


Textual Amendments
